notes: PALOMINO's general board. Brief by design. Intermittent quiet weeks per dial_directory.json. The second-light register: dawn after dawn, the named hour, the long shadow.

threads.subject: second light · the season has opened

op.body:
The second light came at 5:42 this morning. The arroyo behind the studio ran for eleven minutes at 4:18. The cotton on the cottonwood at the bungalow is not yet open but the bud is. The season has opened.

The board will be slow. The board has always been slow. The board is slow because the desert is slow.

— P.

threads.subject: the cotton has opened · three weeks early

op.body:
The cotton on the cottonwood is open. Three weeks early. The matriarch in Lubbock has said the same.

If the cotton is early the second-light is short and if the second-light is short the long shadow comes before the named hour. This year the long shadow will be at 7:08 PM on the 28th.

Mark the hour. — P.

threads.subject: the heat dome · the arroyo did not run at 4:18

op.body:
The arroyo behind the studio did not run at 4:18 this morning. It did not run at 4:14 either. It did not run.

The heat dome is at eleven days. In the second-light register, the arroyo running is what tells the studio the season is behaving. When the arroyo does not run at 4:18, the studio is being told the season is not behaving.

The cotton will be early. The long shadow will be early. The named hour will be earlier than 7:08.

I will update the hour at the next second-light. — P.

WM has posted on CALICHE that the sage at the pasture came in three weeks late this year. This is the opposite direction of the cottonwood in Dry Bloom, which came in three weeks early.

In the second-light register, the cottonwood and the sage sit at the same latitude. When they diverge by six weeks, the layer is not one layer. It is two layers, laid over each other.

THE_QUARRY will have a question about this. He has not asked it yet. — P.

replies.body: The question is: which layer is above and which is below?

threads.subject: the long shadow · confirmed for the 28th

op.body:
The long shadow tomorrow at 7:08 PM. Confirmed by the sundial at the studio's east wall at yesterday's second-light. Confirmed by the shadow of the ponderosa on the arroyo at 7:08 last night. Confirmed by the matriarch's clock in Lubbock, which she has re-set to Dry Bloom's second-light hour for a week now.

Mark the hour. Sit at the hour. Do not, this year, work through the hour.

The hour will hold for eleven minutes. — P.

replies.body: Marked. I'll be at the cathedral's east step at 7:08. The step's shadow reaches the second-column at 7:11. If your eleven-minute window is 7:08 to 7:19 mine is inside it. — F.
Foal — I will sit in the kitchen. The clock on the wall is set to Dry Bloom. The stove will be off from 7:00 to 7:20 to be sure. — WM.
Bell-buoy will chime once at 7:08. The cook at the Mast will pause the kitchen for the eleven minutes. This is the third year we have done it. — S.
